contentBlocks.original:
शिव शिव शक्तिनाथं संहारं शं स्वरूपम्
नव नव नित्यनृत्यं ताण्डवं तं तन्नादम्
घन घन घूर्णिमेघं घंघोरं घंन्निनादम्
भज भज भस्मलेपं भजामि भूतनाथम् ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śiva śiva śaktināthaṃ saṃhāraṃ śaṃ svarūpam
nava nava nityanṛtyaṃ tāṇḍavaṃ taṃ tannādam
ghana ghana ghūrṇimeghaṃ ghaṃghoraṃ ghaṃnninādam
bhaja bhaja bhasmalepaṃ bhajāmi bhūtanātham ..1..
contentBlocks.translation:
I worship Shiva, the Lord of Shakti (power), who embodies destruction and is the very essence of auspiciousness.
He performs the ever-new and eternal cosmic dance (Tandava), creating the primordial sound (nāda).
His presence is like a dark, swirling storm cloud, producing terrifying, deep sounds of thunder.
I worship him, who is smeared with sacred ash (bhasma), the Lord of all beings (Bhoothanatha).

This verse glorifies Shiva in his form as Bhoothanatha, highlighting his destructive, yet auspicious nature, his cosmic dance (Tandava), and his association with sacred ash and primal sound.
contentBlocks.meanings: शिव (śiva) – Shiva; शिव (śiva) – Shiva; शक्तिनाथं (śaktināthaṃ) – Lord of Shakti (śakti:power | nāthaṃ:Lord); संहारं (saṃhāraṃ) – destruction; शं (śaṃ) – auspiciousness; स्वरूपम् (svarūpam) – essence; नव (nava) – new; नव (nava) – new; नित्यनृत्यं (nityanṛtyaṃ) – eternal dance (nitya:eternal | nṛtyaṃ:dance); ताण्डवं (tāṇḍavaṃ) – cosmic dance; तं (taṃ) – that; तन्नादम् (tannādam) – primordial sound (tat:that | nādam:sound); घन (ghana) – dense; घन (ghana) – dense; घूर्णिमेघं (ghūrṇimeghaṃ) – swirling cloud (ghūrṇi:swirling | meghaṃ:cloud); घंघोरं (ghaṃghoraṃ) – terrifying; घंन्निनादम् (ghaṃnninādam) – deep sound (ghaṃ:deep | ninādam:sound); भज (bhaja) – worship; भज (bhaja) – worship; भस्मलेपं (bhasmalepaṃ) – smeared with ash (bhasma:ash | lepaṃ:smeared); भजामि (bhajāmi) – I worship; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Lord of beings (bhūta:beings | nātham:Lord);

contentBlocks.original:
कऴ कऴ काऴरूपं कल्लोऴं कं कराऴम्
डम डम डमनादं डम्बुरुं डंकनादम्
सम सम शक्तग्रिवं सर्वभूतं सुरेशम्
भज भज भस्मलेपं भजामि भूतनाथम् ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaḻa kaḻa kāḻarūpaṃ kalloḻaṃ kaṃ karāḻam
ḍama ḍama ḍamanādaṃ ḍamburuṃ ḍaṃkanādam
sama sama śaktagrivaṃ sarvabhūtaṃ sureśam
bhaja bhaja bhasmalepaṃ bhajāmi bhūtanātham ..2..
contentBlocks.translation:
I worship Shiva, the fierce one with the form of time (Kala), who roars like a tumultuous wave, frightening and terrifying.
He makes the resonating sound of the Damaru drum, creating the powerful, rhythmic beat that reverberates through the cosmos.
He bears the garland of Shakti, the embodiment of power, and is the Lord of all beings and gods.
I worship him, smeared with sacred ash, the Lord of all beings (Bhoothanatha).

This verse highlights Shiva’s fearsome and cosmic form as Kala (time), his association with the Damaru drum, and his role as the supreme lord of all beings and gods, enveloped in the power of Shakti.
contentBlocks.meanings: कऴ (kaḻa) – null; कऴ (kaḻa) – null; काऴरूपं (kāḻarūpaṃ) – form of time (kāḻa:time | rūpaṃ:form); कल्लोऴं (kalloḻaṃ) – tumultuous wave; कं (kaṃ) – null; कराऴम् (karāḻam) – frightening; डम (ḍama) – null; डम (ḍama) – null; डमनादं (ḍamanādaṃ) – sound of Damaru (ḍama:Damaru | nādaṃ:sound); डम्बुरुं (ḍamburuṃ) – null; डंकनादम् (ḍaṃkanādam) – powerful sound; सम (sama) – null; सम (sama) – null; शक्तग्रिवं (śaktagrivaṃ) – garland of Shakti (śakti:Shakti | grivaṃ:garland); सर्वभूतं (sarvabhūtaṃ) – all beings (sarva:all | bhūtaṃ:beings); सुरेशम् (sureśam) – lord of gods (sura:gods | īśam:lord); भज (bhaja) – worship; भज (bhaja) – worship; भस्मलेपं (bhasmalepaṃ) – smeared with ash (bhasma:ash | lepaṃ:smeared); भजामि (bhajāmi) – I worship; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Lord of beings (bhūta:beings | nātham:Lord);

contentBlocks.original:
रम रम रामभक्तं रमेशं रां रारावम्
मम मम मुक्तहस्तं महेशं मं मधुरम्
बम बम ब्रह्मरूपं बामेशं बं विनाशम्
भज भज भस्मलेपं भजामि भूतनाथम् ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rama rama rāmabhaktaṃ rameśaṃ rāṃ rārāvam
mama mama muktahastaṃ maheśaṃ maṃ madhuram
bama bama brahmarūpaṃ bāmeśaṃ baṃ vināśam
bhaja bhaja bhasmalepaṃ bhajāmi bhūtanātham ..3..
contentBlocks.translation:
I worship Shiva, who is dear to Lord Rama and is the Lord of Lakshmi (Ramesha), resounding with the great chant of Ram.
He holds his hand in blessing, offering liberation, and is the great Lord (Mahesha), whose nature is sweet and gentle.
He is the embodiment of Brahman (the supreme reality) and is the Lord of the left-hand path (Bamesha), who destroys all that is impure.
I worship him, smeared with sacred ash, the Lord of all beings (Bhoothanatha).

This verse emphasizes Shiva's connection with Lord Rama, his role as the bestower of liberation, and his manifestation as the ultimate reality, Brahman. It also reflects his power in both creation and destruction.
contentBlocks.meanings: रम (rama) – rejoice; रम (rama) – rejoice; रामभक्तं (rāmabhaktaṃ) – devotee of Rama (rāma:Rama | bhaktaṃ:devotee); रमेशं (rameśaṃ) – Lord of Lakshmi (rama:Lakshmi | īśaṃ:Lord); रां (rāṃ) – chant of Ram; रारावम् (rārāvam) – resounding; मम (mama) – my; मम (mama) – my; मुक्तहस्तं (muktahastaṃ) – hand offering liberation (mukta:liberation | hastaṃ:hand); महेशं (maheśaṃ) – great Lord (mahā:great | īśaṃ:Lord); मं (maṃ) – sweet; मधुरम् (madhuram) – sweet; बम (bama) – left-hand path; बम (bama) – left-hand path; ब्रह्मरूपं (brahmarūpaṃ) – embodiment of Brahman (brahma:Brahman | rūpaṃ:form); बामेशं (bāmeśaṃ) – Lord of left-hand path (bāma:left-hand | īśaṃ:Lord); बं (baṃ) – destruction; विनाशम् (vināśam) – destruction; भज (bhaja) – worship; भज (bhaja) – worship; भस्मलेपं (bhasmalepaṃ) – smeared with ash (bhasma:ash | lepaṃ:smearing); भजामि (bhajāmi) – I worship; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Lord of beings (bhūta:beings | nātham:Lord);

contentBlocks.original:
हर हर हरिप्रियं त्रितापं हं संहारम्
खम खम क्षमाशीऴं सपापं खं क्षमणम्
द्दग द्दग ध्यानमूर्त्तिं सगुणं धं धारणम्
भज भज भस्मलेपं भजामि भूतनाथम् ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hara hara haripriyaṃ tritāpaṃ haṃ saṃhāram
khama khama kṣamāśīḻaṃ sapāpaṃ khaṃ kṣamaṇam
ddaga ddaga dhyānamūrttiṃ saguṇaṃ dhaṃ dhāraṇam
bhaja bhaja bhasmalepaṃ bhajāmi bhūtanātham ..4..
contentBlocks.translation:
I worship Shiva, beloved of Vishnu (Haripriya), who destroys the threefold sufferings (physical, mental, and spiritual) and removes all sorrows.

He is patient and forgiving, pardoning even the sinful, and is the embodiment of compassion and forgiveness.
He is the form of deep meditation, manifesting with attributes (Saguna), and the one who sustains all through deep concentration (dharana).
I worship him, smeared with sacred ash, the Lord of all beings (Bhoothanatha).

This verse reflects Shiva’s close connection with Vishnu, his role as the destroyer of suffering and the merciful forgiver of sins. It also highlights his association with meditation and as the sustainer of all through dharana (concentration).
contentBlocks.meanings: हर (hara) – Shiva; हर (hara) – Shiva; हरिप्रियं (haripriyaṃ) – beloved of Vishnu (hari:Vishnu | priyaṃ:beloved); त्रितापं (tritāpaṃ) – threefold suffering (tri:three | tāpaṃ:suffering); हं (haṃ) – I; संहारम् (saṃhāram) – destruction; खम (kham) – forgiveness; खम (kham) – forgiveness; क्षमाशीऴं (kṣamāśīḻaṃ) – forgiving nature (kṣamā:forgiveness | śīḻaṃ:nature); सपापं (sapāpaṃ) – with sin (sa:with | pāpaṃ:sin); खं (khaṃ) – null; क्षमणम् (kṣamaṇam) – pardoning; द्दग (ddaga) – null; द्दग (ddaga) – null; ध्यानमूर्त्तिं (dhyānamūrttiṃ) – form of meditation (dhyāna:meditation | mūrttiṃ:form); सगुणं (saguṇaṃ) – with attributes (sa:with | guṇaṃ:attributes); धं (dhaṃ) – null; धारणम् (dhāraṇam) – sustaining; भज (bhaja) – worship; भज (bhaja) – worship; भस्मलेपं (bhasmalepaṃ) – smeared with ash (bhasma:ash | lepaṃ:smeared); भजामि (bhajāmi) – I worship; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Lord of beings (bhūta:beings | nātham:Lord);

contentBlocks.original:
पम पम पापनाशं प्रज्वलं पं प्रकाशम्
गम गम गुह्यतत्त्वं गिरीशं गं गणानाम्
दम दम दानहस्तं धुन्दरं दं दारुणम्
भज भज भस्मलेपं भजामि भूतनाथम् ॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pama pama pāpanāśaṃ prajvalaṃ paṃ prakāśam
gama gama guhyatattvaṃ girīśaṃ gaṃ gaṇānām
dama dama dānahastaṃ dhundaraṃ daṃ dāruṇam
bhaja bhaja bhasmalepaṃ bhajāmi bhūtanātham ..5..
contentBlocks.translation:
I worship Shiva, who destroys all sins and radiates with a blazing, brilliant light.
He reveals the hidden truths and mysteries of existence, and is the Lord of the mountains (Gireesha) and the leader of the Ganas (celestial attendants).
He holds the hand of generosity, giving boons, yet remains fierce and formidable in his appearance.
I worship him, smeared with sacred ash, the Lord of all beings (Bhoothanatha).

This verse emphasizes Shiva's role as the destroyer of sins, his illumination of deep spiritual truths, his connection with the Ganas, and his balance between generosity and fierceness.
contentBlocks.meanings: पम (pama) – null; पम (pama) – null; पापनाशं (pāpanāśam) – destroyer of sins (pāpa:sins | nāśam:destroyer); प्रज्वलं (prajvalaṃ) – blazing; पं (paṃ) – null; प्रकाशम् (prakāśam) – brilliant light; गम (gama) – null; गम (gama) – null; गुह्यतत्त्वं (guhyatattvaṃ) – hidden truths (guhya:hidden | tattvaṃ:truths); गिरीशं (girīśam) – Lord of the mountains (giri:mountains | īśam:Lord); गं (gaṃ) – null; गणानाम् (gaṇānām) – of the Ganas; दम (dama) – null; दम (dama) – null; दानहस्तं (dānahastaṃ) – hand of generosity (dāna:generosity | hastam:hand); धुन्दरं (dhundaraṃ) – null; दं (daṃ) – null; दारुणम् (dāruṇam) – fierce; भज (bhaja) – worship; भज (bhaja) – worship; भस्मलेपं (bhasmalepaṃ) – smeared with ash (bhasma:ash | lepaṃ:smeared); भजामि (bhajāmi) – I worship; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Lord of all beings (bhūta:beings | nātham:Lord);

contentBlocks.original:
गम गम गीतनाथं दूर्गमं गं गंतव्यम्
टम टम रूंडमाऴं टंकारं टंकनादम्
भम भम भ्रम् भ्रमरं भैरवं क्षेत्रपाऴम्
भज भज भस्मलेपं भजामि भूतनाथम् ॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gama gama gītanāthaṃ dūrgamaṃ gaṃ gaṃtavyam
ṭama ṭama rūṃḍamāḻaṃ ṭaṃkāraṃ ṭaṃkanādam
bhama bhama bhram bhramaraṃ bhairavaṃ kṣetrapāḻam
bhaja bhaja bhasmalepaṃ bhajāmi bhūtanātham ..6..
contentBlocks.translation:
I worship Shiva, the Lord of music and melody (Gītanātha), who is difficult to reach and the ultimate goal to be attained.
He wears a garland of skulls (Rūṇḍamāla) and resonates with the mighty sound of Ṭanka (a resounding roar).
He moves like a whirling bee (Bhramara) and takes on the fearsome form of Bhairava, the protector of sacred places (Kṣetrapāla).
I worship him, smeared with sacred ash, the Lord of all beings (Bhoothanatha).

This verse highlights Shiva’s role as the Lord of music, his terrifying form adorned with skulls, and his role as Bhairava, the fierce guardian of holy sites, while also likening his movement to that of a bee.
contentBlocks.meanings: गम (gama) – go; गम (gama) – go; गीतनाथं (gītanāthaṃ) – Lord of music (gīta:music | nāthaṃ:Lord); दूर्गमं (dūrgamaṃ) – difficult to reach; गं (gaṃ) – goal; गंतव्यम् (gaṃtavyam) – to be attained; टम (ṭama) – sound; टम (ṭama) – sound; रूंडमाऴं (rūṇḍamāḻaṃ) – garland of skulls (rūṇḍa:skull | māḻaṃ:garland); टंकारं (ṭaṃkāraṃ) – resounding roar; टंकनादम् (ṭaṃkanādam) – mighty sound; भम (bhama) – move; भम (bhama) – move; भ्रम् (bhram) – whirling; भ्रमरं (bhramaraṃ) – bee; भैरवं (bhairavaṃ) – Bhairava; क्षेत्रपाऴम् (kṣetrapāḻam) – protector of sacred places (kṣetra:sacred place | pāḻam:protector); भज (bhaja) – worship; भज (bhaja) – worship; भस्मलेपं (bhasmalepaṃ) – smeared with ash (bhasma:ash | lepaṃ:smeared); भजामि (bhajāmi) – I worship; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Lord of all beings (bhūta:beings | nātham:Lord);

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिशुऴधारी संहारकारी गिरिजानाथम् ईश्वरम्
पार्वतीपति त्वम् मायापति शुभ्रवर्णम् महेश्वरम्
कैऴाशनाथ सतीप्राणनाथ महाकालम् कालेश्वरम्
अर्धचंद्रम् शिरकिरीटम् भूतनाथं शिवम् भजे ॥७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
triśuḻadhārī saṃhārakārī girijānātham īśvaram
pārvatīpati tvam māyāpati śubhravarṇam maheśvaram
kaiḻāśanātha satīprāṇanātha mahākālam kāleśvaram
ardhacaṃdram śirakirīṭam bhūtanāthaṃ śivam bhaje ..7..
contentBlocks.translation:
The wielder of the trident, the one who brings destruction, the Supreme Lord and husband of Parvati (Girija).
You are the consort of Parvati, the Lord of illusion (maya), fair-complexioned, and the great Maheshvara.
You are the Lord of Kailasa, the life force of Sati, the great destroyer (Mahakala), and the master of time (Kaleshvara).
Wearing the crescent moon on your head as a crown, I worship Shiva, the Lord of all beings (Bhoothanatha).

This verse highlights Shiva’s roles as the wielder of the trident and the cosmic destroyer, as well as his compassionate side as the husband of Parvati. He is the Lord of Kailasa and time itself, while the crescent moon on his head symbolizes his control over time and immortality.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रिशुऴधारी (triśuḻadhārī) – wielder of the trident (triśuḻa:trident | dhārī:wielder); संहारकारी (saṃhārakārī) – bringer of destruction (saṃhāra:destruction | kārī:doer); गिरिजानाथम् (girijānātham) – husband of Girija (girijā:Girija | nātham:husband); ईश्वरम् (īśvaram) – Supreme Lord; पार्वतीपति (pārvatīpati) – consort of Parvati (pārvatī:Parvati | pati:husband); त्वम् (tvam) – you; मायापति (māyāpati) – Lord of illusion (māyā:illusion | pati:lord); शुभ्रवर्णम् (śubhravarṇam) – fair-complexioned (śubhra:fair | varṇam:complexion); महेश्वरम् (maheśvaram) – great Maheshvara; कैऴाशनाथ (kaiḻāśanātha) – Lord of Kailasa (kaiḻāśa:Kailasa | nātha:lord); सतीप्राणनाथ (satīprāṇanātha) – life force of Sati (satī:Sati | prāṇa:life force | nātha:lord); महाकालम् (mahākālam) – great destroyer (mahā:great | kālam:time/destroyer); कालेश्वरम् (kāleśvaram) – master of time (kāla:time | īśvaram:lord); अर्धचंद्रम् (ardhacaṃdram) – crescent moon (ardha:half | caṃdram:moon); शिरकिरीटम् (śirakirīṭam) – crown on head (śira:head | kirīṭam:crown); भूतनाथं (bhūtanāthaṃ) – Lord of all beings (bhūta:beings | nāthaṃ:lord); शिवम् (śivam) – Shiva; भजे (bhaje) – I worship;

contentBlocks.original:
नीलकंठाय सत्स्वरूपाय सदा शिवाय नमो नमः
यक्षरूपाय जटाधराय नागदेवाय नमो नमः
इंद्रहाराय त्रिलोचनाय गंगाधराय नमो नमः
अर्धचंद्रम् शिरकिरीटम् भूतनाथं शिवम् भजे ॥८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nīlakaṃṭhāya satsvarūpāya sadā śivāya namo namaḥ
yakṣarūpāya jaṭādharāya nāgadevāya namo namaḥ
iṃdrahārāya trilocanāya gaṃgādharāya namo namaḥ
ardhacaṃdram śirakirīṭam bhūtanāthaṃ śivam bhaje ..8..
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the blue-throated one (Nilakantha), the embodiment of truth and the eternal auspicious one.
Salutations to the one in the form of a Yaksha, with matted locks, and the deity of serpents (Naga).
Salutations to the one adorned with Indra's garland, the three-eyed one (Trilochana), and the one who holds the Ganges (Gangadhara).
Wearing the crescent moon on your head as a crown, I worship Shiva, the Lord of all beings (Bhoothanatha).

This verse focuses on Shiva’s unique attributes: his blue throat, symbolizing his act of drinking the poison during the ocean churning, and his eternal truth as Sada Shiva. Shiva is depicted as a Yaksha, a celestial being, and is connected with serpents and the holy Ganges flowing from his matted locks. The verse again highlights his cosmic powers through his third eye and the crescent moon, establishing him as the Lord of all beings, Bhoothanatha.
contentBlocks.meanings: नीलकंठाय (nīlakaṇṭhāya) – to the blue-throated one (nīla:blue | kaṇṭha:throat | āya:to); सत्स्वरूपाय (satsvarūpāya) – to the embodiment of truth (sat:truth | svarūpa:embodiment | āya:to); सदा (sadā) – eternal; शिवाय (śivāya) – to Shiva; नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; यक्षरूपाय (yakṣarūpāya) – to the form of a Yaksha (yakṣa:Yaksha | rūpa:form | āya:to); जटाधराय (jaṭādharāya) – to the one with matted locks (jaṭā:matted locks | dhara:bearing | āya:to); नागदेवाय (nāgadevāya) – to the deity of serpents (nāga:serpent | deva:deity | āya:to); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; इंद्रहाराय (indrahārāya) – to the one adorned with Indra's garland (indra:Indra | hāra:garland | āya:to); त्रिलोचनाय (trilocanāya) – to the three-eyed one (tri:three | locana:eye | āya:to); गंगाधराय (gaṅgādharāya) – to the holder of the Ganges (gaṅgā:Ganges | dhara:holder | āya:to); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; अर्धचंद्रम् (ardhacandram) – crescent moon (ardha:half | candra:moon); शिरकिरीटम् (śirakīrīṭam) – crown on the head (śira:head | kīrīṭa:crown); भूतनाथं (bhūtanāthaṃ) – Lord of all beings (bhūta:beings | nātha:lord); शिवम् (śivam) – Shiva; भजे (bhaje) – I worship;

contentBlocks.original:
तव कृपा कृष्णदासः भजति भूतनाथम्
तव कृपा कृष्णदासः स्मरति भूतनाथम्
तव कृपा कृष्णदासः पश्यति भूतनाथम्
तव कृपा कृष्णदासः पि वति भूतनाथम् ॥9॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tava kṛpā kṛṣṇadāsaḥ bhajati bhūtanātham
tava kṛpā kṛṣṇadāsaḥ smarati bhūtanātham
tava kṛpā kṛṣṇadāsaḥ paśyati bhūtanātham
tava kṛpā kṛṣṇadāsaḥ pi vati bhūtanātham ..9..
contentBlocks.translation:
By your grace, your devotee Krishnadasa worships Bhoothanatha.
By your grace, your devotee Krishnadasa remembers Bhoothanatha.
By your grace, your devotee Krishnadasa sees Bhoothanatha.
By your grace, your devotee Krishnadasa drinks the essence of Bhoothanatha.

This verse reflects the deep devotion of Krishnadasa. It emphasizes the importance of divine grace in every aspect of a devotee's life and in experiencing the presence of Lord Bhoothanatha (Shiva).
contentBlocks.meanings: तव (tava) – your; कृपा (kṛpā) – grace; कृष्णदासः (kṛṣṇadāsaḥ) – Krishnadasa; भजति (bhajati) – worships; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Bhoothanatha; तव (tava) – your; कृपा (kṛpā) – grace; कृष्णदासः (kṛṣṇadāsaḥ) – Krishnadasa; स्मरति (smarati) – remembers; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Bhoothanatha; तव (tava) – your; कृपा (kṛpā) – grace; कृष्णदासः (kṛṣṇadāsaḥ) – Krishnadasa; पश्यति (paśyati) – sees; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Bhoothanatha; तव (tava) – your; कृपा (kṛpā) – grace; कृष्णदासः (kṛṣṇadāsaḥ) – Krishnadasa; पिवति (pivati) – drinks; भूतनाथम् (bhūtanātham) – Bhoothanatha;

contentBlocks.original:
अथ श्रीकृष्णदासः विरचित ऽभूतनाथ अष्टकम्ऽ यः
पठति निस्कामभावेन सः शिवलोकं सगच्छति ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha śrīkṛṣṇadāsaḥ viracita 'bhūtanātha aṣṭakam' yaḥ
paṭhati niskāmabhāvena saḥ śivalokaṃ sagacchati ..
contentBlocks.translation: Thus, whoever recites the Bhoothanatha Ashtakam composed by Shri Krishnadasa with a desireless mind, will surely attain the abode of Shiva (Shivaloka).
contentBlocks.meanings: अथ (atha) – thus; श्रीकृष्णदासः (śrīkṛṣṇadāsaḥ) – Śrī Kṛṣṇadāsa (śrī:honorific | kṛṣṇadāsaḥ:Kṛṣṇadāsa); विरचित (viracita) – composed; ऽभूतनाथ ('bhūtanātha) – Bhoothanatha; अष्टकम् (aṣṭakam) – octet; यः (yaḥ) – who; पठति (paṭhati) – recites; निस्कामभावेन (niskāmabhāvena) – with a desireless mind (niskāma:desireless | bhāvena:with a mind); सः (saḥ) – he; शिवलोकं (śivalokaṃ) – abode of Shiva (śiva:Shiva | lokaṃ:abode); सगच्छति (sagacchati) – will attain;
